what_it_does: Executes the operator-approved RG9 write-back. Loads reconciled.json and candidates.json, takes the confirmed-class entries, reconstructs each into the registry_writeback contract shape (address, represents, howto, capabilities, maps_to_feature, mockup_file, outerHTML pulled from the candidate, run/model stamps), then runs a SAFETY PARTITION against the live design/_system/addresses.json: new addresses and byte-identical existing ones go to the safe set, addresses that differ on key fields are held back as curated conflicts. It calls registry_writeback(safe, write=True, skip_already_stamped=True), records Tim's verbatim approve verdict on s107-review via inbox.resolve, and returns a report (submitted/added/skipped/stamped/conflicts-held/unbuildable/total-after).
observations: Imports registry_writeback as wb (sys.path includes design/_system)
TIM_VERDICT constant holds Tim's verbatim 2026-06-10 approval quote
Operates only on entries with class=='confirmed'
candidate_of() resolves a candidate from sources[0] mockup via the rg10-batch artifact's confirmed/flagged sets and cluster_members[0], caching artifacts in arts dict
Entries that can't resolve a candidate go to unbuildable (reported, not written)
Writeback entry stamps run='rg10-refined' and model='chat-4b (floor+prose+panel gated)'

Safety partition compares on cmp_keys=(region, represents, howto, capabilities, maps_to_feature); identical existing entries kept in safe (merge is idempotent), differing ones held as conflicting with differs_on list
registry_writeback called with write=True, skip_already_stamped=True; every write is git-committed per docstring
Returns counts derived from report['merged'] statuses (added/skipped) plus stamped/conflicts/unbuildable/registered_after
standouts: Records a verbatim human-conversation verdict as a constant tied to a specific inbox item (s107-review)
Safety partition refuses to fold a curated-content collision into a block-approve — conflicting curated entries become their own decision
All-or-nothing, fail-loud, git-committed write-back delegated to the existing registry_writeback module (reuse-don't-parallel)
Mentions 385 confirmed entries in docstring (this is the actual apply run)
conventions: Reuses registry_writeback rather than reimplementing merge/stamp/re-parse
Same path-bootstrap + main()->dict + __main__ json.dumps(default=str) pattern as sibling scripts
Stamp every written entry with run + model provenance
concerns: candidate_of has nontrivial index logic (cluster_members[0] into the per-mockup candidate slice) that silently yields unbuildable if the index doesn't line up
wb._dossier_to_entry is a private (underscore) function called from outside its module
Bare json.load(open(...)) on several files with no missing-file handling
Hardcoded model string and run label as literals
notes: The RG9 apply step: takes operator-approved confirmed dossiers, partitions out curated-content collisions, writes only the safe set through the existing registry_writeback (git-committed), and records Tim's verbatim approve verdict on s107-review.
questions: What exactly does registry_writeback's merge/stamp/re-parse do and what is its report schema (merged/stamped/registered_after)?
Why does the docstring reference 385 entries while sibling surface/walk scripts reference different counts (190/463) — are these different run snapshots?
What is region (a cmp_key) and where does it come from since it isn't in the dossier shape seen in the batch files?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — performs the operator-approved write-back with a curated-conflict safety partition and verdict recording, exactly as the docstring describes.
apparent_themes: Approval-authorizes-action (the verdict is the trigger and is recorded with provenance)
Safety partition / never overwrite curated content silently
Provenance stamping on every generated entry
intention_signals: Trying to safely grow the address registry from an approved autonomous run while guaranteeing that nothing the operator curated is overwritten without a separate explicit decision, and that the human approval is durably recorded alongside the write.
novelty: The pre-write safety partition that separates new/identical/conflicting against live curated entries, and binding the write to a stored verbatim operator verdict.
contribution: Contributes the approved write-back runner: dossier→writeback-contract reconstruction, a curated-conflict safety partition, delegation to registry_writeback, and verdict recording on the originating inbox item.
summary_for_embedding: Python script (RG9) that applies operator-approved confirmed RG10 dossiers to the address registry via registry_writeback after a safety partition that holds back curated-content conflicts, and records Tim's verbatim approve verdict on s107-review.
intention_for_embedding: To safely write an approved autonomous registry run into the live address registry without clobbering curated entries, recording the operator's approval as the authorizing, reversible action.
